title: The __name__ == '__main__' Pattern
description: Master the if __name__ == '__main__' idiom that makes Python files work both as importable modules and standalone scripts. Build functions to detect main guards in source code, extract the guarded block, automatically add a guard, and check if a script is properly runnable.
starterCode:
def has_main_guard(code_text):
    """Check if Python source code contains an if __name__ == '__main__' guard.

    Looks for a line matching the pattern:
        if __name__ == '__main__':
    or with double quotes:
        if __name__ == \"__main__\":

    Returns True if found, False otherwise.
    """
    pass


def extract_main_block(code_text):
    """Extract the code inside the if __name__ == '__main__' block.

    Returns the body of the main guard (the indented lines after the
    if statement) as a single string with the indentation preserved.
    Returns an empty string if no main guard exists.
    """
    pass


def add_main_guard(code_text, main_func_name):
    """Add a main guard to the end of the given code text.

    Appends:
        \n\nif __name__ == '__main__':
            <main_func_name>()

    If the code already has a main guard, return it unchanged.
    """
    pass


def is_runnable_script(code_text):
    """Determine if a Python source text is a runnable script.

    A script is considered runnable if:
      1. It has a main guard, AND
      2. The function called in the main guard is defined in the code

    Returns True if runnable, False otherwise.
    """
    pass
hints: Use a regex like: if\s+__name__\s*==\s*['"]__main__['"]\s*: to match the main guard with either single or double quotes.
For extract_main_block, after finding the guard line, collect indented lines until you hit a non-indented line (or end of file).
For add_main_guard, check has_main_guard first — if it already exists, return code_text unchanged.
For is_runnable_script, extract function calls from the main block and check if they appear in def statements elsewhere in the code.

testCases.description: has_main_guard detects the guard with single quotes
has_main_guard returns False when no guard exists

testCases.validation:
code = "def main():
    pass

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
    print('done')"; result = extract_main_block(code); assert 'main()' in result and 'print' in result

testCases.description: add_main_guard appends guard or returns unchanged if exists
testCases.validation: code = 'def run():\n    pass'; result = add_main_guard(code, 'run'); assert "if __name__" in result and 'run()' in result; assert add_main_guard(result, 'run') == result

testCases.description: is_runnable_script returns True when function is defined and called in main guard
testCases.validation:
code = "def main():
    print('hello')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()"; assert is_runnable_script(code) == True

testCases.description: is_runnable_script returns False when called function is not defined
